title: Vendor/mainline non-SDMMC fabric snapshot
kernel_subject: mmc: snapshot A733 fabric registers during IDMAC stall

test_setup.vendor_read: dd if=/dev/mmcblk0 of=/dev/null bs=4K count=8192 iflag=direct status=none

comparison.interpretation: MBUS root gate/config matches at the sampled phase.
Vendor keeps the IOMMU0 MBUS/APB sys gates enabled even though SDMMC0 is not listed as an IOMMU master.
Vendor keeps MSI-lite0 bus gate enabled; mainline SDMMC0 currently consumes MSI-lite1 only.
The prior MSI-lite1 mapping diagnostic matches vendor state for this gate.
Vendor keeps MSI-lite2 bus gate enabled; this was not covered by the MSI-lite1-only test.
Vendor keeps IOMMU1 MBUS/APB sys gates enabled.

comparison.delta: mainline keeps only AHB store bit 24 from this broad vendor envelope
comparison.interpretation: Broad delta includes many non-SDMMC domains; do not copy the whole value into upstream or lab DTS.

comparison.delta: mainline lacks IOMMU0/1, DESYS, VID_IN, and MSI-lite2-side bits
comparison.interpretation: Safe next test should isolate only MSI/IOMMU-relevant bits first, not display/video bits.

comparison.delta: mainline lacks DMA0, CE, and GMAC0 MBUS gates
comparison.interpretation: Do not enable GMAC0 as part of the next SDMMC test; Ethernet remains out of scope.
Not first H021 target because the mainline PIO rail and card init are stable at 25 MHz, while the failure is descriptor fetch.
SDMMC0 bus gate/reset line matches vendor at the sampled phase.

conclusion: H020 found a concrete non-SDMMC delta: vendor keeps a broader fabric/MSI/IOMMU clock envelope active while mainline leaves most of that envelope disabled. The delta is too broad to copy wholesale because it includes display/video and GMAC-related bits. Queue H021 as a lab-only safe subset that enables only MSI-lite and IOMMU sys/fabric bits missing from mainline, while explicitly avoiding Ethernet, VPU/display, and DTS changes.
